data.title: Stock futures mixed, Nasdaq up, Producer Prices edge down 0.1% in August. Fed likely to cut rates in Sept. Dow -0.27%, SN500 0.13%, Nasdaq 0.07%. Core inflation up 2.8%. Bond yields: 10yr 4.08%, 2yr 3.54%. SPY up 0.51%, QQQ up 0.53%. US stocks at record highs on revised job data. Labor market showed signs of slowing earlier than expected. Labor Dept revised job figures, removed 911,000 jobs between April 2024-March 2025. 911,000 nonfarm payroll jobs removed between April 2024-March 2025 indicating cooling labor market. Market dipped briefly post data release.
data.description: U.S. stock futures on Wednesday showed mixed results following previous advances, with the Producer Price Index slightly decreasing due to a 0.2% drop in prices for final demand services, while demand goods increased by 0.1%. Core inflation saw a 0.3% rise in prices, with a 2.8% increase over the past year. Stocks hit record highs on Tuesday as revised U.S. job figures indicated a slowing labor market, boosting confidence in a potential Federal Reserve interest rate cut in September. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.08%, and the two-year bond stood at 3.54%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool showed markets foreseeing a 100% chance of a rate cut in the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting. In premarket trading, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust rose 0.51% to $653.65, and the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF increased by 0.53% to $583.56. Sectors like utilities, health care, and communication services saw gains, while materials and industrials fell. The Bureau of Labor Statistics revised nonfarm payroll jobs, indicating an earlier labor market decline than expected. This led to all major indices closing at record highs on Tuesday.

data.title: 911,000 U.S. jobs erased, raising concerns of AI automating tech jobs, possible market stall, and fear of stagflation.
data.description: A recent update by the Bureau of Labor Statistics has led to the removal of 911,000 previously reported jobs in the United States, causing concern in economic circles and solidifying expectations for an upcoming interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The revision for the year through March 2025 has raised doubts about the strength of the labor market and the impact on the record-breaking market rally. Analysts are warning that this adjustment could introduce fears of stagflation. Specific weakness was noted in the tech sector, with insights pointing to AI automation as a factor in job loss. Bill Adams, Chief Economist of Comerica Bank, highlighted the information industry as particularly affected. The revised data indicate a trend of tech job automation. Additionally, significant cuts in the leisure sector were also identified. This information suggests potential challenges ahead for the job market and the economy at large.

data.title: Shah's Halal Food partners with MetLife Stadium, offering three food locations to Giants and Jets fans.
data.description: Amityville, N.Y., September 12, 2025 - In a recent announcement, Shah's Halal Food disclosed a collaboration with MetLife Stadium, the renowned venue for the New York Jets and New York Football Giants. This exciting partnership has resulted in the introduction of three upscale food service venues within the stadium, offering authentic Halal cuisine from NYC. This initiative aims to enhance the culinary experience for sports enthusiasts and attendees at the stadium.

data.title: Viña Santa Rita's Casa Real launches 2022 vintage celebrating 30 vintages of excellence on La Place de Bordeaux.
data.description: On September 11, 2025, the renowned Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon Casa Real from Viña Santa Rita introduced its latest vintage of 2022 in New York. This marks the 30th exceptional vintage produced by the iconic wine, showcasing a legacy of excellence. The wine is crafted exclusively in notable years, representing a tradition of quality and distinction.

data.description: Mohammad Abedini was involved in providing navigation technology for Iranian Shahed drones, which have been utilized in various conflicts globally.

data.title: CUPE flight attendants rally at Parliament Hill on 9/16 to end unpaid work in Canada's airline industry, working average 35 hours/month for free.
data.description: Flight attendants from various Canadian airlines, under the representation of CUPE, are set to rally at Parliament Hill on September 16th, urging the government to address the prevalent issue of unpaid work in the country's airline sector. The recent conflict at Air Canada highlighted the problem of unpaid labor, sparking concerns nationwide. On average, flight attendants dedicate 35 hours monthly without compensation for crucial safety tasks, prompting calls for action against this widespread practice.

data.title: Ryan named to Crain’s Chicago Business Best Places to Work list for 7th year in a row. Ginny B. Kissling gives credit to Chicago team members.
data.description: In Chicago, Ryan, a prominent global tax services and software provider, has been honored by being included in the prestigious Crain’s Chicago Business 2025 list of Best Places to Work for the seventh consecutive year. This achievement speaks volumes about the dedication and excellence of the Chicago team members at Ryan. Ginny B. Kissling, the President Americas and Chief Operating Officer, attributed this recognition to the team's passion, collaborative approach, and unwavering dedication to clients. This recognition underscores Ryan's commitment to maintaining a positive work environment, showcasing its consistency in providing outstanding workplace experiences.
